rulings.Case Title: HEMLATA G. VYAS VS. INTERSPACE INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Of Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 28, 2013, line 6. Plaintiff's Re-Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Of Complaint DENIED. No proof of service on file and motion was filed untimely per CCP 473(b). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 28, 2013, line 7. Plaintiff's Re-Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Of Complaint DENIED. No proof of service on file and motion was filed untimely per CCP 473(b). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN E. WAI VS. MIME, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 28, 2013, line 1. PLAINTIFF JOHN WAI's Motion For Entry Of Judgment GRANTED as follows: Plaintiff shall recover $4,500 in outstanding settlement balance; $575 in attorney's fees; and $510 in costs. Plaintiff fails to indicate Stipulation provision allowing interest and basis for calculation of interest. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DEBORA SILVERMAN VS. MURPHY SAN FRANCISCO LIMITED PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 28, 2013, line 2. DEFENDANT MURPHY SAN FRANCISCO LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, MURPHY INVESTMENTS, INC. and BARTHOLOMEW MURPHY's Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Protective Order DENIED. Issues regarding depositions of Bartholomew and Dennis Murphy have been addressed in this Court's previous orders. Issues regarding terminating sanctions as to 1st and 11th causes of action are not supported by points and authorities. No sanctions. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SHEILA HARRIS-YOUNG et al VS. BAY AREA PROPERTIES, LTD. et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 28, 2013, line 3. DEFENDANT BAY AREA PROPERTIES, LTD.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. (1) Demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to 9th causes of action for UCL violation for Plaintiff to allege standing. (2) Demurrer is OVERRULED re: statute of limitations. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges that habitability issues have never been addressed and are still ongoing. (3) Demurrer to 2nd cause of action for IIED is OVERRULED, Plaintiff sufficiently alleges all elements, including outrageous conduct and severe emotional distress. (4) Demurrer is OVERRULED as to 5th cause of action for breach of covenant of quiet enjoyment. Complaint sufficiently alleges substantial interference with tenancy in 14. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SHEILA HARRIS-YOUNG et al VS. BAY AREA PROPERTIES, LTD. et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 28, 2013, line 3. DEFENDANT BAY AREA PROPERTIES, LTD.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT DENIED as follows: (1) as to punitive damages - complaint sufficiently alleges facts re: "conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others." (2) as to injunctive relief - moot, in light of ruling on demurrer. = (501/REQ)

Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 28, 2013, line 4. DEFENDANT RHONDA FINCH's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT DENIED, 5 days to answer. Issues of fact are improperly raised. = (501/REQ)

Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 28, 2013, line 4. DEFENDANT RHONDA FINCH's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint and "3 Day Notice" sufficiently allege a cause of action for unlawful detainer based on at least unauthorized sublet. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: YUK WA WONG VS. TONY CARME et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 28, 2013, line 5. DEFENDANT TONY CARME and TY MUEHLER's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT GRANTED with leave to amend to properly verify. No opposition filed. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JENNY GEE VS. OSCAR AVILA et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 28, 2013, line 8. DEFENDANT OSCAR AVILA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege relationship between Plaintiff and Defendant supporting a cause of action for Unlawful detainer. Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that "owner" (4) and "subsequent occupant" (6c(3)) is one of such relationships. = (501/REQ)
